title: brief-003 realization revision: replace SessionStart-hook activation with CLAUDE.md-primer instruction (router arms Monitor at session start)
description: Test-harness BC diagnosed a hook-model mismatch in brief 003's canonical templates: Claude Code awaits SessionStart hooks synchronously, so a foreground 'inotifywait -m' invocation never returns and startup hangs / times out. Schema + host prereqs are fine; the mismatch is structural — synchronous hook delivery is incompatible with a long-running watcher. Brief 003's two invariants (every shop reactive on session start; activation honors brief 001 encapsulation) still hold. The realization changes: in place of a SessionStart hook running inotifywait, the canonical CLAUDE.md primer carries an explicit instruction telling the router (Claude session) to arm the Monitor tool on a 'stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to <watch-dir>/' pipeline at session start. Monitor IS the in-session primitive that delivers the 'background process whose stdout streams as notifications' semantic the hook was faking. This bead carries the revision: PO authors superseding scenarios (numbered 71+, tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates) that retire the hook-model-bound scenarios (57/58/59/65/68 wholesale; 70 keeps as schema-conformance with shrunk scope) and pin the new realization (CLAUDE.md primer per shop type contains activation-instruction substring; instruction names host prereqs + failure mode; bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md contains the instruction). Architect handles the request_bugfix dispatch carrying this round.
notes:
PO authored 6 new scenarios at features/templates/71-76 (Scenario Outlines where shop-type-parametrized, single Scenarios where shop-type-specific). Tagged @bc:shopsystem-templates at dispatch time by Architect. Hash computation deferred to Architect per role contract.

Retire-with-successor map:
- 57 (d4b8aac19b34ea8c, BC-side 1621b59b0ea8b20b) → superseded by 73 (bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md carries activation instruction); settings.json package-data surface survives but is no longer the activation home.
- 58 (2a09d13c21d48aa0, BC-side 679d227f04533ad4) → superseded by 71 (canonical CLAUDE.md primer contains activation-instruction substring).
- 59 (21769e2a22c36bc7, BC-side c1e7f31eeef73e05) → superseded by 75 (canonical lead settings.json declares 'bd prime' as sole SessionStart inner-hook, no activation hook).
- 65 (a9379ab3a162158d, BC-side a9379ab3a162158d) → superseded by 72 (activation instruction names prereqs + refuse-with-diagnostic failure mode).
- 68 (a3338bb773fda65e, BC-side 287e6a4f31533336) → superseded by 74 (canonical bc settings.json carries no SessionStart entries; subsumes 68's 'no other-shop-type watch target' clause).
- 70 (f2a7de49d80332c1, BC-side f2a7de49d80332c1) → superseded by 76 (schema conformance conditional on SessionStart being present; bc-variant antecedent is vacuously satisfied).

Keep unchanged: 60, 61, 62 (bootstrap/update mechanics for settings.json — still apply byte-for-byte to the new canonical contents pinned by 74 and 75); 63, 64 (CLAUDE.md primer + bootstrap-generated CLAUDE.md name host prereqs — substrings inotify-tools/coreutils/inotifywait/stdbuf still required by router's Monitor invocation, motivation now consistent with 72); 66, 67 (editable-install discipline, unaffected); 53 (CLAUDE.md references bd discipline, parallel structural pin to 73 for activation).

PO stance committed on the open question (canonical bc.json shape): option (b) — bc.json ships as a minimal hooks wrapper with no SessionStart entries. Rationale: preserves managed-update workflow (scenarios 60/61/62 still apply byte-for-byte to the new content); leaves the slot available for future terminate-cleanly BC-side hooks without committing speculative content now; avoids the destructive cost of retiring 60/61/62 wholesale (option a) and avoids the speculative-content failure mode of shipping hooks before a use case exists (option c). lead.json ships with 'bd prime' as the sole SessionStart inner-hook (the prior two-hook composition under scenario 59 collapses to a one-hook shape since the activation hook is gone).

No intent gaps surfaced during authoring. The realization revision is internally consistent: 71+72 pin the new realization home (CLAUDE.md primer), 73 pins it surviving the bootstrap pour, 74+75 pin the now-cleared-out settings.json shape per shop type, 76 carries forward the schema-conformance invariant under the new constraint. Architect handles request_bugfix dispatch carrying this round; the dispatch description should instruct the Implementer to retire the 6 listed BC-side scenarios when landing 71-76, mirror the supersedes-via-Feature-description-block convention established in scenario 69.

title: request_bugfix: brief 003 implementation (12 scenarios 57-68, post-supersession re-dispatch)
description:
Re-dispatch of brief 003's 12 scenarios (57-68) to shopsystem-templates, carried as request_bugfix per BC's earlier vehicle observation (BC already has bootstrap capability; brief 003 extends it).

BACKGROUND
==========
The lead-xkh chain hit a coordination gap. Sequence:

- lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, 12 scenarios 57-68 for brief 003: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json, bootstrap extension) → BC clarified on contradiction with prior-pinned scenario 51 (compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin in bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature).
- lead-5mz (request_bugfix, carried only scenario 69 — the supersession removing scenario 51's settings.json clause, leaving AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) → BC work_done complete, scenario 1a6e90189f9c2ade attested, supersession landed clean.

But: lead-5mz carried ONLY scenario 69. Brief 003's 12 positive pins (.claude/settings.json writing, prereqs naming, venv-sync, etc.) were never delivered to the BC. lead-xkh still has a clarify in its outbox, so the BC's router will not re-process it. To get brief 003 implemented, the lead must send a NEW dispatch carrying the 12 brief 003 scenarios.

This is a protocol gap shape: the §4.4 closure assumption that lead-5mz's terminal work_done closes lead-xkh's chain doesn't hold because lead-5mz only carried the supersession scenario, not the brief 003 positive pins. Prior chains (lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e) worked because each follow-up bundled the prior scenarios. The lead-5mz dispatch instruction was wrong on this — it should have bundled.

LINKAGE
=======
- Predecessors: lead-xkh (assign_scenarios for brief 003, clarify-stalled in BC outbox), lead-5mz (closed; clean supersession of scenario 51).
- lead-xkh remains in_progress with a pointer to this new bead. lead-xkh closes implicitly when this new work_id's terminal work_done arrives in the BC outbox.

SUPERSESSION STATE
==================
- Prior scenario 51 (hash 7d64bb8ed5a3f656, compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin) is retired. Its settings.json clause was removed by lead-5mz.
- New scenario 69 (hash 1a6e90189f9c2ade, AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) lives in BC features/bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature.
- Brief 003's scenario 60 (bootstrap writes canonical .claude/settings.json per shop type) no longer conflicts with anything in the BC — the contradiction lead-xkh tripped on is resolved.

PAYLOAD (12 scenarios 57-68)
============================
Implements brief 003 items A–E:

- A (canonical .claude/settings.json templates ship from shop-templates): scenarios 57, 68.
- B (SessionStart inotifywait hook, four reference-realization properties): scenarios 58, 65.
- C (lead-shop settings.json composes with existing bd prime hook): scenario 59.
- D (bootstrap/update writes and re-pours .claude/settings.json; CLAUDE.md names activation host prereqs): scenarios 60, 61, 62, 63, 64.
- E (lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv; bootstrapped shop resolves BC CLI invocations to live source on session start): scenarios 66, 67.

Scenario files (lead canonical authored locations):
  features/templates/57-canonical-claude-settings-json-templates-ship-from-shop-templates.gherkin
  features/templates/58-canonical-claude-settings-json-declares-sessionstart-hook.gherkin
  features/templates/59-canonical-lead-settings-json-composes-with-existing-bd-prime-hook.gherkin
  features/templates/60-bootstrap-writes-canonical-claude-settings-json-per-shop-type.gherkin
  features/templates/61-update-re-pours-claude-settings-json-as-managed-file.gherkin
  features/templates/62-update-is-idempotent-on-already-current-claude-settings-json.gherkin
  features/templates/63-bootstrap-generated-claude-md-names-activation-host-prereqs.gherkin
  features/templates/64-canonical-claude-md-primer-names-activation-host-prereqs.gherkin
  features/templates/65-canonical-sessionstart-fails-loudly-on-missing-inotifywait.gherkin
  features/templates/66-bootstrapped-shop-resolves-bc-cli-invocations-to-live-source-on-session-start.gherkin
  features/templates/67-lead-bootstrap-installs-sibling-bc-clones-editable-into-product-venv.gherkin
  features/templates/68-canonical-bc-settings-json-does-not-name-lead-watch-target.gherkin

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (previously prepared, surfaced here since assign_scenarios was retired in favour of request_bugfix and request_bugfix's description IS the carrier for procedural framing)
=========================================================================================================================================================================================

- Scope item E realization framing: scenario 66 is the outcome pin (BC CLI invocations resolve to live source under repos/ on session start). Scenario 67 is the mechanism pin (lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv). The two compose: 67 is one valid realization of 66, but 66 is the durable contract the Reviewer attests against, and 67 names the specific mechanism the canonical templates encode today. If a future realization replaces editable installs with a different live-source resolution (e.g., a path-aware shim), 66 remains intact and 67 gets superseded.

- Scenario 65 (loud-fail on missing inotifywait): the canonical SessionStart hook MUST produce a visible failure when inotifywait is missing on PATH; silently degrading to a no-watcher state is the failure mode this scenario pins against. The canonical settings.json should encode this property via the hook's startup command shape (e.g., exit-on-missing-binary), not via a separate runtime probe.

- Scenario 59 (lead settings.json composition): the lead-shop's canonical settings.json composes both the inotifywait activation hook AND the bd prime hook on SessionStart. Concretely the SessionStart entry has multiple hook commands, not a single replaced command. This is so the activation hook does NOT clobber bd prime (operational hygiene).

PROCEDURAL REMINDERS
====================
- Hash discipline via wire-form per lead-wgv: the shop-msg send CLI computes hashes from the wire-form scenario body. Pass --scenario-file flags only; do not pre-hash.
- venv activated per lead-zmi: source .venv/bin/activate before invoking shop-msg.
- Vehicle is request_bugfix per BC's earlier vehicle observation in the lead-xkh clarify: BC already carries bootstrap pins; brief 003 tightens/extends the existing bootstrap behavior with additional managed files and CLAUDE.md content. This is NOT new capability — bootstrap exists and writes managed files today; this dispatch adds .claude/settings.json to the managed set and extends the CLAUDE.md content the bootstrap writes.

EMPIRICAL PRE-STATE VERIFICATION
================================
- Verified BC's bootstrap capability already exists by inspecting BC features/bootstrap_cli_surface.feature, bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature, and bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature: scenarios for bootstrap writing managed agent files, writing CLAUDE.md, writing .gitignore, and invoking bd init are all pinned and green per lead-5mz's regression pin list. So Q1 (does the BC have the capability?) answer is YES — bootstrap exists, writes managed files; brief 003 extends the managed set.
- Verified BC does NOT pin .claude/settings.json today: grep '.claude/settings.json' across BC features/ produces no hits after lead-5mz landed. So the specific extension brief 003 names is unpinned — Q2 (does any scenario currently pin it?) is NO. Tightening, not flat change.
- Therefore: request_bugfix is the correct vehicle.
close_reason: BC work_done complete: 129/129 BDD passing, all 12 dispatched hashes attested (1621b59b0ea8b20b, 679d227f04533ad4, c1e7f31eeef73e05, f83e03ee69261242, d29cd723439faae1, d3066d4476d0a975, cad1153ef4dfd18c, 6bc3eb5f62115d91, a9379ab3a162158d, 3957f255c35aff60, ff882696856530a4, 287e6a4f31533336). 6 mutation probes confirmed teeth + 3 direct empirical probes (loud-fail hook under PATH=/nowhere, _install_sibling_bc_clones_editable with no repos/, update mtime preservation). Prior pins regression: all 9 examples green. Post-state spot-check empirically verified: claude_settings/{bc,lead}.json package-data exist with stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait pipelines; cli.py carries _CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PKG constant + read_claude_settings_template() + bootstrap pour at line 365 + update re-pour at line 588 + _install_sibling_bc_clones_editable() at line 390; bootstrap probe of fresh BC target produced .claude/settings.json byte-identical to bc.json template.

title: request_bugfix: scenario 69 (no top-level AGENTS.md, supersedes 51's .claude/settings.json clause)
description:
Parent: lead-xkh (brief 003 / scenarios 57-68). Child dispatch closing

Origin

title: scenarios 57-68: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json + bootstrap extension (brief 003)
description:
Brief 003 (lead-0h1) — Event-driven shop activation. 12 scenarios extending the
shop-templates bootstrap surface to ship a canonical `.claude/settings.json`
per shop type plus host-prereq naming, and to install sibling BC clones
editable from the lead bootstrap.

The brief carries two invariants and five scope items:

- Invariant 1: every shop is reactive on session start (BC watches own
  `inbox/`; lead watches `repos/*/outbox/`).
- Invariant 2: activation honors brief 001's encapsulation — the
  watcher fires a wake signal; reading content still goes through
  `shop-msg`.
- Scope A: canonical `.claude/settings.json` per shop type (scenarios 57, 68).
- Scope B: SessionStart hook content with stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait,
  alias-bypassed flushing filter, merged 2>&1 for startup-chatter
  visibility, monitor-mode persistent arming (scenarios 58, 59, 65).
- Scope C: bootstrap writes settings.json; update re-pours it as a
  bootstrap-managed file (scenarios 60, 61, 62).
- Scope D: prereq naming (`inotify-tools`, `coreutils`, `inotifywait`,
  `stdbuf`) in bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` and the canonical primer
  (scenarios 63, 64).
- Scope E: live-source resolution outcome + lead bootstrap installs
  sibling BC clones editable into the product venv (scenarios 66, 67).

## Empirically validated context

This session validated the stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait pipeline end-to-end
on lead-79q reconciliation: sub-second event-fire on `work_done`
arrival via the reference pipeline named in scope item B. The pattern
works. Scope items A/B/C/D codify the canonical shape so every
bootstrapped shop gets it by default.

## Pre-state cited

Pre-state verification on shopsystem-templates current state:

- Package data at `src/shop_templates/templates/`: 4 role-prompt files
  (`bc-implementer.md`, `bc-reviewer.md`, `lead-po.md`, `lead-architect.md`)
  + 2 CLAUDE.md primers under `templates/claude/` (`bc.md`, `lead.md`) +
  `gitignore.template`. No `.claude/settings.json` template exists.
- `src/shop_templates/cli.py`: bootstrap writes `.claude/agents/*.md`,
  top-level `CLAUDE.md` (from primer + `{{SHOP_NAME}}` substitution),
  top-level `.gitignore`, and invokes `bd init --skip-agents`. No
  `.claude/settings.json` is written. `update` re-pours managed
  agent files only — no settings.json handling.
- Empirical bootstrap of a probe directory
  (`shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name probe
  --target /tmp/probe-003`) produced exactly: `CLAUDE.md`,
  `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md`, `.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`.
  No `.claude/settings.json`. Exit 0.
- `repos/shopsystem-templates/features/` scan: no existing scenarios
  pin SessionStart hooks, settings.json content, Monitor harness,
  inotifywait, or venv install mode.

All 12 scenarios target net-new capability — `assign_scenarios` is the
correct vehicle.

## Implementation notes (informational; bodies are the contract)

- Scope item E is realization-pinned by the PO via two scenarios:
  scenario 66 pins the outcome (live-source resolution on session
  start) and scenario 67 pins the install-shape (lead bootstrap
  installs sibling clones editable into the product venv). Scenario 67
  alone is the PO's leaning shape; the Implementer is free to add a
  hook-time refresh on top if pre-state on E surfaces drift cases not
  covered by 67 alone, or to treat 67 as sufficient.

- Scenario 65 pins the "loud fail on missing `inotifywait`" property.
  The Implementer must select a hook shape such that a host with no
  `inotifywait` on PATH surfaces a visible diagnostic — not a silent
  no-watcher state. A polling fallback that silently activates
  violates the invariant; if the Implementer commits a fallback, it
  must itself be a visible notification.

- Scenario 59 specifies the canonical lead variant **composes** with the
  existing `bd prime` `SessionStart` hook — two distinct entries under
  `SessionStart`, not a replacement.

- Scope D's prereq naming surface is the Implementer's call between
  `CLAUDE.md` primer (named in scenarios 63 + 64 as the realization)
  vs other places. The scenarios commit primer + bootstrap-generated
  CLAUDE.md as the surface; the Implementer must satisfy both.

## Procedural reminders

- Hash discipline: per `lead-wgv`, verify computed hashes against
  wire form, not against the body source pre-CLI. The `shop-msg send`
  CLI computes hashes from canonicalized wire form; the Implementer's
  BDD harness must read from the same wire form so verification holds.

- Mailbox encapsulation: `shop-msg` is the only sanctioned mailbox
  boundary on both sides. Do not hand-edit mailbox YAML; do not `cat`
  it. If `shop-msg pending` fails on a stale venv, fix the install —
  do not bypass the CLI (per `lead-xq0`).

- Bead ID: `lead-xkh` is the lead-shop work_id for this assignment.
  No dotted suffix; non-dotted lead-shop bead IDs per `lead-4wy`.

title: request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates: scenario 46 closes lead-bwu clarify (per-step shop-msg read outbox in lead-architect 'Responding to BC clarify' subsection)
description:

Parent: lead-bwu (request_bugfix carrying 9 scenarios for brief lead-231 items B+D). Grandparent: lead-231 (brief 001).

Trigger: lead-bwu's clarify (BC Implementer/Reviewer in shopsystem-templates) flagged an asymmetry between lead-architect.md line 272 (bare 'Read the clarify' opening step in 'Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond' subsection — no shop-msg read outbox cite on the same step) and lead-po.md lines 160-162 (explicit 'Read the BC's clarify via shop-msg read outbox --bc-root <BC root> --work-id <work_id>' form). Scenario 56b3ef5f48342b9b (= features/templates/20) pins template-wide invariants but does not pin per-step CLI naming inside procedural subsections.

Router scope ruling: in scope of brief lead-231 items B+D. Invariant 1 of brief 001 ('shop-msg is sole inter-shop messaging surface; consumers never touch storage directly') is per-step discipline; a Constraints clause is too weak to prevent regression.

Vehicle: request_bugfix. Discriminator:
- Q1 (capability exists?): YES. Empirically verified by reading repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/lead-architect.md line 272 and lead-po.md lines 160-162; the asymmetry exists exactly as the clarify describes. Lead-architect 'Sending a message' subsection lines 261-266 already names shop-msg read inbox explicitly, demonstrating the BC template's authors knew the explicit form — the omission in the Responding subsection is a gap, not a pattern.
- Q2 (pinned?): NO at the procedural-step granularity inside the Responding subsection.
- Q3 (behavioral or flat?): behavioral — tightens existing unpinned discipline. Behavioral → request_bugfix.

Carries: features/templates/46-lead-architect-responding-to-clarify-subsection-names-shop-msg-read-outbox-per-step.gherkin (PO-authored; tightened beyond the BC's draft to add a third Then condition pinning that path-shape references like 'outbox/', '<BC root>/outbox/', 'the outbox file' also cannot appear without naming the CLI on the same step).

§4.4 closure signaling: when shopsystem-templates emits work_done on THIS work_id, that work_done implicitly sign-offs the original 9 scenarios dispatched under lead-bwu (per shop-system §4.4 loop closure shape). The BC should NOT emit a separate work_done on lead-bwu retroactively. The dispatch description names this expectation.

Implementer breadth expectation: scenario 46 pins the lead-architect 'Responding to a BC clarify' subsection, but the underlying invariant (per-step shop-msg read outbox naming wherever a procedural step reads outbox storage) is template-wide. Implementer should scan all four canonical templates for the asymmetry pattern, not just edit the cited subsection.

title: assign_scenarios from lead (lead-yy30)
notes:
Lead work_id: lead-yy30
clarify: ARCHITECT RULING (lead-yy30, grounded in ADR-021 + ADR-018 + the committed scenario-40 declarative-shape precedent). Per-scenario deliverable artifact, tracked path, and pin layer follow. The governing precedent is ALREADY committed in scenario 40 (lead-usc7/40c, clarify lead-jx4u): a no-Actions-runner, in-memory-fake BDD harness cannot exercise a LIVE workflow run or a LIVE ghcr registry, so infra/CI behavior is pinned by inspecting the committed workflow/Dockerfile as a DECLARATIVE ARTIFACT (structural file-tree + YAML inspection), with live-registry/live-Actions/credential-permission state explicitly OUT-OF-BAND and NOT asserted in-suite. ADR-018 forbids any pin that requires executing live infra. Apply that precedent uniformly.

(1) d9909f38 (Dockerfile repo-artifact; = scenario 36). Deliverable: an ACTUAL committed Dockerfile at a tracked path in the bc-launcher repo that builds shopsystem-bc-base. Pin layer: STRUCTURAL file-tree inspection. Steps inspect (a) the file exists at the tracked path, (b) it installs the framework CLIs in the "github.com/dstengle/<utility> @ vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH" VCS/published-package pin shape (not editable-from-clone). No registry, no build execution. This is the same shape as your existing shared-postgres scenarios bb070f75/8ad37d1a that conftest tests structurally.

(2) b688a5fe (publish-to-ghcr; scenario 37), 4e470f7 (rebuild-on-repository_dispatch; scenario 38), be11d615 (rollback re-tag; scenario 41). Deliverable: committed .github/workflows/*.yml in the bc-launcher repo. Pin layer: STRUCTURAL YAML inspection — NOT a live-registry assertion and NOT a new in-src registry-driver fake. Do NOT stand up an in-src registry seam to simulate digest/tag resolution for these three; that would be testing a fake's own arithmetic, not the deliverable. Instead pin: (37) a publish workflow triggered on push of tags matching "v*" that builds and pushes to ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base tagging both the version and "latest" with public visibility declared; (38) the SAME (or a sibling) workflow declares a "repository_dispatch" trigger whose job rebuilds and re-pushes "latest"; (41) rollback is the already-published-digest re-tag observable — pin it as the declarative property that the publish workflow tags by version (so prior digests remain pullable) enabling a "latest"-repoint; if no rollback automation is authored, a workflow-shape/README-runbook declarative pin of the re-tag procedure suffices. The live-registry "same digest", "D_new distinct from D_old", "unauthenticated docker pull", and "successful Actions run" clauses are OUT-OF-BAND (scenario-40 precedent): the in-suite proxy is the declared workflow structure, not a live pull.

(3) af2f03d3 (launch resolves latest digest before run; = scenario 39). THIS ONE IS DIFFERENT. It is NOT infra/CI — it is in-process launch BEHAVIOR your harness CAN and SHOULD pin behaviorally. Deliverable: launch gains an explicit digest-resolution step before container start, injected via a new RegistryDriver protocol that is fakeable in tests. The fake simulates the registry resolving "latest" -> D_new; the test asserts launch calls resolve, pulls/uses D_new, and the started container runs from D_new not the cached D_old. This is a real in-src seam with a behavioral test — the right tool here precisely because the behavior lives in controller.launch, not in a workflow file.

SEAM-SHARING ANSWER (your explicit question): NO. Scenarios (2) and (3) do NOT share one RegistryDriver. (2) is workflow/CI side, pinned declaratively as committed YAML — no in-src seam at all. (3) is launch-path side, pinned behaviorally via the new RegistryDriver protocol. The RegistryDriver protocol belongs to scenario 39 ONLY. Introducing a registry-driver fake to "test" 37/38/41 is the failure mode ADR-018 warns against — it tests the simulator, not the contract artifact.

Summary deliverables: 36 -> committed Dockerfile (tracked path) + structural inspection. 37/38/41 -> committed .github/workflows/*.yml + structural YAML inspection, live state out-of-band. 39 -> launch digest-resolution step + new RegistryDriver protocol fakeable in tests, behavioral assertion. No ADR amendment needed: this is ADR-021 + scenario-40 precedent applied, not new policy.

NOTE on hash drift: the hashes you cite (d9909f38/b688a5fe/4e470f7/be11d615/af2f03d3) are the DISPATCHED bodies; lead-held bodies for 36/37/38/39/41 have since been edited and recompute to af9e04c7167018d0/82f553ba0511089b/2ba5f928d0bb5039/dd18538ac68045ce/06766294e0e9ca72. Pin against the bodies as DISPATCHED to you (the hashes in your inbox payload); a lead-side re-sync bead is filed separately. Do not re-derive from drifted lead-held text.

title: No consume-inbox command + supersession does not drain BC inbox → stale/stranded messages accumulate
description: Two related shop-msg gaps observed 2026-06-02. (1) Superseding a dispatch (lead-ybxs→lead-fksz→lead-tk0f to shopsystem-templates) closes the beads but leaves the superseded request_bugfix MESSAGES pending in the BC inbox forever — templates BC saw all three (ybxs/fksz live+stale) on session-start. (2) There is no CLI path to remove a message from one's own inbox: 'shop-msg consume' supports only the outbox subcommand (lead consuming BC responses). So the misdelivered lead-pw41 copy stranded at the lead root, and superseded predecessors, cannot be drained without hand-editing storage. Need either a 'consume inbox'/'withdraw' command or supersession semantics that withdraw the prior message on re-send. Relates to lead-0217 (registry misdelivery).

The audit scope (other CLI paths that scope bd cwd to a registry path) remains the open work; the central hardening provides the foundation. lead-gf4h stays OPEN per dispatcher instruction.

title: dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-xsmn)
notes:

- message_type: request_bugfix
- scenarios (3, block-hash → wire-hash):

- retirement targets (block hashes; BC's @scenario_hash entries are WIRE hashes the BC pinned at original assign-time and must locate via grep -r '@scenario_hash' features/):
    scenario 16 (16-launch-with-startup-prompt.gherkin): 3e327a468829c3a6 (superseded by 27)
    scenario 07 (07-inject-sends-prompt-to-tmux-session.gherkin): 0d94fa9eb9f5197f (superseded by 28)
- sibling bead: lead-hyee (parallel inject-side bug, same root cause; resolved by scenario 28 in this dispatch).
- Pre-state verification (artifact-surface per ADR-018): bc-container launch --help and bc-container inject --help confirm the entry points exist; the same-day empirical reproduction on lead-xsmn's description is cited in the dispatch description.
- DO NOT CLOSE on dispatch; closure on work_done arriving for lead-xsmn (which will also close lead-hyee).
WORK_DONE RECEIVED 2026-05-30 (lead-architect reconciliation):

- Hash-register validation: PASS. All three carried hashes (0e733774844ed9f3, 17518db1dc1c9001, 5ef728039884a9a2) match the lead-side dispatch records.
- Origin/main landing: PASS. git ls-remote refs/heads/main → f76fe4ea3c377fb2dcce5e7728a5b24323ee7345 (matches BC-claimed HEAD).
- Retirement: BC reports wire hashes 76228a581318f90c (scenario 16 / bc_container.feature:54 inject) and a6162bd63fca8ed4 (:128 launch) removed by title+body match; orphaned Then defs removed. ADR-018 makes the BC's gated-loop reporting admissible evidence — accepted.
- BDD: 117 passed, 0 failed (BC-reported).

EMPIRICAL RE-TEST OUTCOME: FAIL — DO NOT CLOSE.

The BC's SRC-ALREADY-CORRECT framing is contradicted by observable behavior at f76fe4e:

1. Upgraded lead .venv to bc-launcher@f76fe4e (pip install --force-reinstall --no-deps git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-launcher.git@f76fe4e; pip show confirmed shopsystem-bc-launcher 0.1.0).
2. Launched shopsystem-docs (BCLAUNCHER_HOST_HOME=/home/dstengle bc-container launch shopsystem-docs --repo-url ... --shopmsg-dsn postgres://...messaging-postgres-1.../shopsystem --network shopsystem).
3. Launcher printed "Injected startup prompt: '...'" (success path).
4. bc-container monitor shopsystem-docs at +5s and +20s: prompt visible in tmux input buffer (❯ prefix), agent NOT engaged. No ● response, no ✻ thinking indicator.
5. bc-container inject shopsystem-docs "" (empty-inject workaround): agent immediately engages and begins processing the buffered prompt.

This is the SAME behavior the router reproduced this morning at caf62f5. The fix the BC claims was already present at lead-3oi/lead-9sq's commits is not effective end-to-end. The BDD layer (FakeDockerDriver) faithfully models the send-keys API call but not the tmux+Claude TTY semantics that ultimately determine whether Claude Code's input buffer commits.

FOLLOW-UP FILED: lead-9q0f (P1 BUG-DOWNSTREAM) now BLOCKS lead-xsmn. lead-xsmn cannot close until the downstream behavior is fixed and re-tested. lead-hyee also remains OPEN (cross-linked via related-to lead-9q0f).

This is the right outcome under ADR-018: the BC's BDD-gated-loop is admissible evidence for the artifact (send-keys API) but the lead's empirical re-test against the actual CLI artifact surface (live bc-container launch → tmux → Claude) is admissible evidence at a higher integration level and supersedes when they conflict.

Re-test environment: container bc-shopsystem-docs (stopped after test), tmux session 'agent', Claude Code v2.1.157.

Memory check: 'project_launcher_fast_patches_uncommitted.md' was already SUPERSEDED 2026-05-28 (d02f5b9 canonical). No update needed — f76fe4e is now the head but is empirically not yet a complete fix. 'feedback_autonomous_launcher_mission.md': the mission is "stop when bc-container launch + send-work works end-to-end" — empirical re-test shows launch does NOT work end-to-end yet, so the mission stays active.

ROOT-CAUSE FOLLOW-UP DISPATCHED 2026-05-30 via lead-lez1.

The prior lead-xsmn dispatch's BDD-layer fix was empirically refuted at f76fe4e (see prior notes). lead-lez1 dispatches the actual downstream fix as request_bugfix to shopsystem-bc-launcher with scenarios 30 (launch path) and 31 (inject path) pinning the two-discrete-send-keys-invocations shape. Scenarios 27 (wire 0e733774844ed9f3) and 28 (wire 17518db1dc1c9001) — the ones this bead originally dispatched — are scheduled for retirement on landing of lead-lez1. Scenario 29 (wire 5ef728039884a9a2) is correct as-is and stays.

Wire hashes for the superseding scenarios:
- 30 (launch): 6477b2ab3720ac53
- 31 (inject): ad68aaf60377706e

DO NOT CLOSE on dispatch. Closure when lead-lez1 closes (work_done arrives + lead-side empirical re-test passes).
close_reason: Closed as sibling of lead-lez1 (root-cause fix verified empirically at bc-launcher 9cbb9c1). lead-xsmn was the original launch-path bug dispatch whose SRC-ALREADY-CORRECT work_done was refuted by lead-9q0f's diagnostic and ultimately resolved by lead-lez1's two-discrete-send-keys-invocations fix. title: dispatch request_bugfix -> shopsystem-messaging (lead-mxxm)
notes:
mechanism_observation received from shopsystem-messaging (2026-05-30):

Subject: "Dropping shop_root existence guard (ADR-018) exposed an uncaught crash in the ADR-017 bd side-effect path"

Summary: The lead-mxxm dispatch framed scenarios 45-47 as warning-suppression on name-addressed ops. In practice the deleted existence guard in _cmd_pending_inbox was MASKING a second defect — bd_facade.bd_available(cwd) calls bd list with cwd scoped to a registry-derived shop_root; subprocess raises FileNotFoundError/NotADirectoryError (NOT the BdFacadeError callers catch) when that path is absent. Same latent crash sits on the ADR-017 BC-side-bead respond path at cli.py:1240 (_apply_bc_bead_response), not just the lead-mxxm site at cli.py:435 (_cmd_pending_inbox).

BC's in-line remediation: hardened bd_available centrally to return False when cwd is not a directory — covers both observed sites and future call sites that route through it.

Architect disposition (lead-architect, 2026-05-30):
- (a) Filed fresh bead lead-gf4h to track the proposed_action: audit other CLI paths that scope bd cwd to a registry path and confirm they route through hardened bd_available rather than calling _run_bd directly. P2, suggested vehicle request_maintenance once audit identifies stragglers.
- Did NOT extend lead-2id (respond collision) or lead-7wp (lost dispatches); the latent-crash audit is doctrinally distinct (ADR-017/ADR-018 surface coherence) and warrants its own bead trail.
- Did NOT close lead-mxxm; closure remains on its own work_done arrival per standing rule. work_done has not yet arrived.
- Did NOT dispatch a follow-up request_bugfix. Observation reports the in-line fix as already applied in the BC working tree; the lead-mxxm work_done will carry the bd_available hardening as a side-effect of the scenarios 45-47 implementation. Confirm via scenario register on reconciliation. If the work_done summary explicitly references the bd_available hardening, the audit bead lead-gf4h can proceed; otherwise file a clarify on whether the hardening landed.
- ADR-018 doctrine note: this observation strengthens the case that ADR-018's "no refusal on absent shop_root" contract surface implicates every ADR-017 side-effect site, not only the read-time warning text. Worth a one-line addendum to ADR-018 if a future revision pass touches it; not load-bearing enough to file as a standalone doc bead.
close_reason: Hash register matched (82e88384ccd417d8, dac49bb9814f7ddc, 33221aa5431226f3); origin/main landed at ef2dc83bd45bdca498ac132400064004e86beb51 (dispatch baseline 120713f); empirical re-test passed — BC-addressed shop-msg read/pending inbox ops complete cleanly with no 'registry may be stale' or 'does not exist on disk' warnings, even though lead host carries no repos/ BC source. BC summary explicitly references bd_available hardening landed via cli.py + bd_facade.py + conftest.py + feature on ef2dc83 — implicitly verifies the lead-gf4h latent crash hardening since any uncaught FileNotFoundError on the BC-addressed call paths would have surfaced during empirical re-test (it did not).

ROOT CAUSE OF BC's BLOCKED REPORT: the BC ran its BDD suite against the same stale installed package present on this lead host. title: PDR-010 file 121: presence heartbeat collapsed into shop-msg watch
description:
Dispatch PDR-010 implementation feature file 121 to shopsystem-messaging as assign_scenarios (5 scenarios pinning ADR-014). shop-msg watch --bc <name> UPSERTs a bc_presence row on a 30s tick cadence; shop-msg bc-status reads the table and classifies each BC by age of last_seen_at (online <90s, stale 90s-5min, offline >5min). The watch process IS the liveness signal; no separate heartbeat daemon.

DEPENDS ON file 119 (lead-tuu5) landing first: bc_presence schema migration and CLI side-effect integration ride on the bd_facade + atomicity foundation.

title: PDR-010 file 120: dispatch dependencies via bd dep, honored by shop-msg send

Dispatch PDR-010 implementation feature file 120 to shopsystem-messaging as assign_scenarios (6 scenarios pinning ADR-013). shop-msg send consults the bd depends-on graph before depositing a postgres outbox row; strict mode (default) refuses sends whose predecessor is not closed; queued mode (--queue-on-dependency) writes a bd-side queued entry promoted to live dispatch on predecessor close.

title: carrier: lead-nkd5 — upgrade installed shop-templates to >=39a64d7 before re-pour
description:
## Carrier dispatch resolving lead-nkd5 clarify

The shopsystem-docs Implementer flagged that the BC's installed
'shop-templates' package is built from commit 3f74c52 (per its
dist-info direct_url.json), NOT the lead-named canonical commit
39a64d7. Its packaged templates therefore match the lead-nkd5 documented
PRE-STATE — running 'shop-templates update' would silently no-op and
fail acceptance criteria 1 and 2. The canonical source path
/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates does not exist
in the BC's container, and PDR-009 forbids hand-copying.

## Architectural answer: option (b)

Lead-side precondition: BC upgrades its installed shop-templates package
to at least canonical commit 39a64d7 (effectively, reinstall from
shopsystem-templates main / 39a64d7) BEFORE running the re-pour. PDR-009's
'use mechanism, do not hand-copy' stance is intact; the precondition is
that the installed mechanism's packaged content matches the lead-named
commit.

Verification step the Implementer can run:
- python -c "import json,pathlib; print(json.loads(pathlib.Path([p for p in __import__('sys').path if 'site-packages' in p][0]+'/shop_templates-0.1.0.dist-info/direct_url.json').read_text())['vcs_info']['commit_id'])"
  must report a commit at or beyond 39a64d7 in shopsystem-templates main.
- Equivalently, in the dist-info directory of the python env that supplies
  'shop-templates' on /usr/local/share/nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin:/home/vscode/.local/bin:/usr/local/share/nvm/current/bin:/usr/local/python/current/bin:/usr/local/py-utils/bin:/usr/local/jupyter:/usr/local/share/nvm/current/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/share/nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin:/usr/local/python/current/bin:/usr/local/py-utils/bin:/usr/local/jupyter:/usr/local/share/nvm/current/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/home/vscode/.local/bin, cat direct_url.json and read vcs_info.commit_id.

## Corrected acceptance criteria

1. The installed shop-templates package's dist-info vcs_info.commit_id
   is >=39a64d7 in shopsystem-templates main (precondition). If the BC's
   container ships a stale build, the BC reinstalls before re-pour
   (e.g. 'pip install --force-reinstall git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-templates@39a64d7'
   or equivalent; the exact recipe depends on how the BC's image
   provisions shop-templates).
2. After re-pour via 'shop-templates update --shop-type bc':
   - .claude/agents/bc-implementer.md: wc -l == 474; grep -c 'Pre-emit step' == 2.
   - .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md: grep -c 'YOUR FIRST ACTION' >= 1;
     grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} == 2 (design invariant per PDR-003 alt F,
     NOT == 0; matches lead-glig carrier guidance).
   - .claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md: wc -l == 298.
   - .claude/settings.json: byte-for-byte identical to canonical (no
     re-pour needed; harmless if invoked).
3. No hand-copying or hand-substitution of any kind (PDR-009 + PDR-003
   alt F).

## Cross-links

- lead-nkd5 (original dispatch this resolves)
- lead-hgc7 (captured mechanism observation; closed as filed)
- lead-glig (sibling launcher carrier; same dispatch pattern, different
  underlying concern — {{SHOP_NAME}} substitution there, package commit
  lag here)

## Dispatch plan

Dispatch via shop-msg send request_maintenance to shopsystem-docs with
this work_id, naming the precondition explicitly and reproducing the
corrected acceptance criteria above.
notes: Dispatched 2026-05-29 via shop-msg send request_maintenance --bc shopsystem-docs --work-id lead-kwn0. Deposit verified by shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-docs --work-id lead-kwn0.
close_reason: Re-pour delivered byte-identical to shopsystem-templates@39a64d7 at origin/main:10c4cfd. Verified: bc-implementer.md=474L w/ 2x Pre-emit step; bc-primer.md=170L w/ 2x YOUR FIRST ACTION and {{SHOP_NAME}} preserved (PDR-003 alt F); bc-reviewer.md=298L. Package-bump precondition (3f74c52->39a64d7) executed via user-site shop-templates update before re-pour. Outbox work_done consumed.

title: [mechanism] re-pour installed shop-templates package commit lag breaks PDR-009 (lead-nkd5 obs)
description:
## Mechanism observation from shopsystem-docs (lead-nkd5)

Surfaced during lead-nkd5 re-pour. The docs BC Implementer observed
that the PDR-009 re-pour mechanism has no contract binding the installed
'shop-templates' package's build commit to the canonical commit the
lead names in the dispatch.

## Empirical evidence

In the shopsystem-docs container:
- Installed shop_templates dist-info direct_url.json -> vcs_info.commit_id
  = 3f74c52 (predates 39a64d7).
- Packaged templates/bc-implementer.md: 305 lines, 0 'Pre-emit step'
  (matches lead-nkd5 documented PRE-STATE, NOT target 474 / 2).
- Packaged templates/claude/bc.md: 0 'YOUR FIRST ACTION' (NOT >=1
  target for bc-primer).
- /workspaces/.../repos/shopsystem-templates does not exist in BC env;
  no 474-line bc-implementer.md anywhere on the BC's filesystem.

## Why load-bearing, not item-specific

Running 'shop-templates update' would silently no-op and exit 0. A less
careful implementer following 'use the mechanism, do not hand-copy'
would emit work_done believing acceptance was met, when in fact every
count assertion would fail. The acceptance criteria's exact wc -l /
grep -c checks are the only catch; the mechanism itself provides no
commit-provenance guard. This will recur on every future re-pour
dispatch where the BC's installed shop-templates lags the lead-named
canonical commit.

## Proposed action (carried verbatim from observation)

(a) Re-pour dispatches should pin/verify the installed shop-templates
    package commit against the message's named canonical commit before
    pour; or
(b) Lead bumps the BC's installed package to the named commit as a
    precondition; or
(c) The mechanism could surface its own build commit (e.g.
    'shop-templates version --commit') so the implementer can assert it
    matches before pouring.

## Lead-side resolution for lead-nkd5 (carrier dispatch follows)

The immediate fix for lead-nkd5 is option (b) — the BC upgrades its
installed shop-templates to >=39a64d7 (or main) before running the
re-pour. Carrier dispatch will name this precondition explicitly in
the corrected acceptance criteria. The general mechanism gap remains
open in this bead.
close_reason: Filed as captured mechanism observation. Lead-side resolution for lead-nkd5 is option (b) — BC bumps installed shop-templates to >=39a64d7 before re-pour — delivered via the carrier dispatch. The general mechanism gap (no commit-provenance contract in the re-pour mechanism) remains a follow-up: surface 'shop-templates version --commit' so implementers and dispatches can assert match before pour. Closing as 'filed'.

title: chore
description:
## Carrier dispatch resolving lead-eanv clarify

The shopsystem-devcontainer Implementer flagged that lead-eanv's
acceptance criteria #1 ('modulo {{SHOP_NAME}} -> shopsystem-devcontainer
substitution') and #2 ('grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} returns 0') contradict
PDR-009's no-hand-copy mechanism, because shop-templates update writes
canonical files byte-for-byte WITHOUT substitution per PDR-003 alt F
(see shop_templates/cli.py:216-228 _render_claude_md comment).

Empirical: post-update grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} bc-primer.md == 2, matching
the pre-state evidence. Prior lead-8yl re-pour landed identically.

## Architectural answer: option (a)

Acceptance #2 was misstated. The expected post-state is:
- grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md == 2
  (NOT == 0)
- No hand-substitution of {{SHOP_NAME}}.
- Shop identity remains in .claude/shop/name.md ('shopsystem-devcontainer'),
  resolved at CLAUDE.md include time per PDR-003 alt F.

PDR-009's no-hand-copy stance and PDR-003 alt F's no-substitution stance
are mutually consistent; the lead-eanv dispatch's acceptance language
imposed a substitution that neither PDR requires.

## Corrected acceptance criteria

1. bc-primer.md, bc-implementer.md, bc-reviewer.md match the canonical
   .claude/canonical/<file> and .claude/agents/<file> in
   shopsystem-templates@39a64d7 byte-for-byte (modulo none — no
   substitution required).
2. grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} .claude/canonical/bc-primer.md returns 2 (the
   design invariant post-pour per PDR-003 alt F).
3. bc-implementer.md grep -c 'Pre-emit step' returns 2 (lead-8lm/ke1/cw7
   fixes landed).
4. bc-reviewer.md is ~298 lines (matching canonical, lead-dl6 expansion).
5. settings.json untouched (already identical pre-pour).

## Mechanism

shop-templates update --target . --shop-type bc, run from the
shopsystem-devcontainer working tree. No hand-substitution.

## Cross-links

- Resolves clarify on: lead-eanv
- Structural finding: lead-w5s5 (mechanism observation about
  {{SHOP_NAME}}-substitution discriminator)
- Design reference: PDR-003 alt F
- Mechanism reference: PDR-009
notes: Dispatched 2026-05-29 via shop-msg send request_maintenance --bc shopsystem-devcontainer --work-id lead-glig. Deposit verified via shop-msg read inbox. Resolves lead-eanv clarify with option (a) (acceptance #2 misstated; PDR-003 alt F retains {{SHOP_NAME}} post-pour).
close_reason: Carrier dispatch complete. Verified shopsystem-devcontainer commit 83e0c56 on origin/main: three re-poured files match templates@39a64d7 byte-for-byte (bc-primer.md vs claude/bc.md; bc-implementer.md vs bc-implementer.md; bc-reviewer.md vs bc-reviewer.md). Invariants confirmed: grep -c '{{SHOP_NAME}}' bc-primer.md == 2 (PDR-003 alt F); grep -c 'Pre-emit step' bc-implementer.md == 2; bc-reviewer.md == 298 lines; bc-implementer.md == 474 lines. Outbox consumed.

title: chore
description:
## Mechanism observation from shopsystem-bc-launcher (lead-xq7z)

Surfaced during lead-xq7z re-pour. The launcher BC Implementer observed
that lead-xq7z's two acceptance requirements were mutually incompatible
by design:

1. "Use the canonical re-pour mechanism (shop-templates CLI), do NOT
   hand-copy."
2. "grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} bc-primer.md returns 0, with {{SHOP_NAME}} ->
   shopsystem-bc-launcher substitution applied."

Per shop_templates/cli.py:216-228 (the _render_claude_md comment),
PDR-003 alt F intentionally writes the primer body byte-for-byte with
no shop-specific substitution. Shop name lives in .claude/shop/name.md,
resolved at CLAUDE.md include time. Post-pour grep -c {{SHOP_NAME}} == 2
is the design invariant, NOT a defect.

Confirmed empirically 2026-05-29:
- shopsystem-product/.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md → 2 {{SHOP_NAME}}
- shopsystem-bc-launcher/.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md → 2 {{SHOP_NAME}}

## Why load-bearing, not item-specific

This shape will recur on every primer re-pour against either shop type.
A lead-architect drafting acceptance for a re-pour is likely to read
{{SHOP_NAME}} as a placeholder needing fill-in. The implementer then
faces a contradiction between "use canonical mechanism" and "grep -c
'{{SHOP_NAME}}' returns 0", with no way to satisfy both.

Same pattern just produced contradictory acceptance in lead-eanv
(devcontainer re-pour); resolved via carrier dispatch in lead-rtf
(or whichever ID the follow-up takes).

## Proposed action

Two non-exclusive options for follow-up:

1. **Lead-architect template tightening.** Add a sentence to the
   request_maintenance vehicle guidance: when drafting a primer
   re-pour acceptance, do not require {{SHOP_NAME}} substitution out
   — PDR-003 alt F retains it post-pour. Verify pre-state by
   checking shop_templates/cli.py:_render_claude_md, not by reading
   {{SHOP_NAME}} as a placeholder.

2. **Revisit PDR-003 alt F.** If the broad expectation is that primers
   should not carry literal placeholders post-pour, the canonical
   mechanism should perform the substitution. The current asymmetry
   (shop name in .claude/shop/name.md vs. literal {{SHOP_NAME}} in
   primer header) is confusing to first-touch readers.

Either resolution would prevent future request_maintenance dispatches
from carrying contradictory acceptance criteria of this shape.

## Evidence

- shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/cli.py:216-228 (no-substitution
  design comment)
- shopsystem-product/.claude/canonical/lead-primer.md:1,3 (lead shop's
  own primer carries {{SHOP_NAME}})
- shopsystem-bc-launcher/.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md:1,3 (BC primer
  carries {{SHOP_NAME}} post-pour)

## Cross-links

- Surfaced by: lead-xq7z (launcher re-pour)
- Same shape hit: lead-eanv (devcontainer re-pour, clarify)
- Design reference: PDR-003 (alt F)
- Mechanism reference: PDR-009 (re-pour vehicle)
close_reason: Filed as captured mechanism observation. Two follow-up paths open: (1) lead-architect template guidance tightening for primer re-pour acceptance drafting; (2) reconsider PDR-003 alt F substitution stance. Closing as 'filed'; either follow-up will reference this finding when prioritized.

title: [mechanism] screen-scraped readiness markers must be unambiguous across UI states (TUI launcher discipline)
description:
Mechanism observation from lead-5ig (work_id: lead-5ig-obs-input-ready-marker-overlap).

## Observation

While implementing lead-5ig, the BC empirically verified that the bare '❯' glyph is NOT a discriminating Claude Code post-trust input-ready marker — it also appears in the PRE-trust pane as the trust-prompt selector arrow:

  Accessing workspace:
  /tmp/empirical_trust_test
  Quick safety check: ...
  ❯ 1. Yes, I trust this folder      <- '❯' here, PRE-trust
    2. No, exit

With CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER='❯' and the new PRE-trust CLAUDE_READY_MARKER='Accessing workspace:', step 4's wait would succeed trivially on the same pane content that satisfied step 2 — silently bypassing the trust-accept Enter and re-creating the lead-3oi deadlock signature in a different form.

## Resolution in lead-5ig

CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER was set to 'bypass permissions on' (footer-line text only present once trust prompt has cleared AND --dangerously-skip-permissions is active). Pinned by test_claude_input_ready_marker_is_unambiguously_post_trust.

## Load-bearing knowledge

This is a property of how Claude Code v2.1.x renders its TUI, not specific to lead-5ig. The next dispatch that introspects the same panes would reproduce the bug if it took marker suggestions at face value.

## Proposed action (lead-shop side)

Add a note to the lead-architect template's pre-state-verification guidance: when picking a screen-scraped readiness marker for a TUI-driven launcher, verify the marker token does not also appear in adjacent UI states (PRE-trust vs POST-trust panes both contained '❯').

Template path candidate: repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/lead-architect.md (or canonical equivalent) — under the 'verify BC pre-state empirically' guidance block.

## Evidence

- src/bc_launcher/controller.py:39 (CLAUDE_INPUT_READY_MARKER) at BC commit 69e1ee3
- Empirical pane capture 2026-05-29 (devcontainer)
- BC pinning test: test_claude_input_ready_marker_is_unambiguously_post_trust

## Cross-link

Observed during lead-5ig (closed). This bead captures the durable mechanism finding so a lead-architect template amendment can be sequenced separately from the lead-5ig fix itself.

title: [finding] lead-primer.md is reactive-only, lacks continuous-action stance — symmetric to lead-00b
description:
Sibling finding to lead-00b. Same passivity pattern observed on the LEAD side during 2026-05-28/29 sessions.

Setup: I drove dispatches and reconciliations when user prompted ('process the work', 'launch the docs', 'what are we doing'). Between user prompts I idled even with visible next work — in-flight BC dispatches (lead-7v1, lead-gua, lead-yaj, lead-qfc all in_progress since 2026-05-22, no follow-up); pending reconciliations stuck in inbox until lead-bp3 ships (lead-0bw, lead-33r); new Monitor events processed only because they fired as task notifications. User feedback verbatim: 'It seems like this session is pretty passive as well. How can we improve the prompts to continually bias towards action.'

Root cause hypothesis: lead-primer.md (and bc-primer.md per lead-00b) has 'Standing rule: reacting to Monitor events' framing — reactive. Has no equivalent 'Standing rule: between Monitor events, drive ready work.' The Monitor arming + drain are session-start; nothing covers continuous-action stance during the session.

Three candidate amendments to BOTH lead-primer.md and bc-primer.md:

(1) END-OF-TURN CONTINUATION RULE. After completing any unit of work, the router MUST identify and start the next ready action before declaring the turn done. Idle only when no ready action exists. Equivalent prose for both primers, scoped to their respective ready-action surfaces (BC: inbox drain + work execution; lead: outbox drain + bd ready + in-flight stale-check + reconciliation).

(2) IDLE-DETECTION CHECKLIST. Concrete list to walk before declaring idle. Lead-side: shop-msg pending outbox/inbox; bd in_progress filtered by 'no activity >24h'; bd ready unblocked beads; cross-link any reconciliation marked blocked but unblocker now resolved. BC-side: shop-msg pending inbox; bd ready in-BC; in-flight work_id with no commit on origin/main.

(3) CHOICE-SUPPRESSION RULE. Standing rule: do not surface procedural choices to the user ('path 1 / path 2 / your call'). Pick the action, act, report what you did. Surface choices only for scope/vocabulary decisions that require user judgment.

Lead-00b sibling: lead-ke1 covers the bc-primer.md tightening. This bead covers the lead-primer.md tightening AND the symmetric (1)/(2)/(3) framework that should apply to both.

Live surface: every lead session that doesn't have continuous user prompting will accumulate stale dispatches and unprocessed Monitor events. The reactive-only stance can't catch its own stalls.

Recommendation: bundle with lead-ke1 as a single broader request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates covering both primers + the three new standing rules. Architect should fold or supersede lead-ke1 as appropriate.
notes: Dispatching as request_bugfix to shopsystem-templates: three amendments (end-of-turn continuation rule, idle-detection checklist, choice-suppression rule) applied symmetrically to BOTH claude/bc.md and claude/lead.md. Pre-state: grep confirms lead.md:94 'Standing rule: reacting to Monitor events' is reactive-only; bc.md has YOUR FIRST ACTION (just-landed lead-ke1) but no continuous-action standing rule. Cross-link lead-ke1 (just closed, commit 5dcc8cd) — templates BC may consolidate diff against fresh primer state.
close_reason: Closed

title: dispatch to shopsystem-bc-launcher: default --startup-prompt to session-start imperative
description:
Child of lead-00b. Vehicle: request_maintenance to shopsystem-bc-launcher.

Pre-state (verified empirically):
- repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/cli.py:30 declares: p_launch.add_argument('--startup-prompt', help='Text to inject into tmux after start')
- repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/cli.py:135 wires it: startup_prompt=getattr(args, 'startup_prompt', None)
- When caller omits the flag, controller receives None and no synthetic first prompt is injected. Result: BC tmux Claude Code instance sits at empty prompt awaiting human input even with SessionStart hooks running.

Change requested: when --startup-prompt is not supplied, default it to a minimal session-start imperative (e.g., 'Run your session-start sequence per /workspace/CLAUDE.md (arm Monitor on shop-msg watch, then drain pending inbox via shop-msg pending inbox), then await user direction.') so bc-container launch <bc> injects that prompt automatically. Explicit --startup-prompt 'foo' must still override.

Acceptance:
- bc-container launch <bc> with no --startup-prompt injects the default imperative into tmux.
- bc-container launch <bc> --startup-prompt 'foo' still uses 'foo'.
- Default text vocabulary stays in lockstep with bc-primer.md (arm Monitor, drain pending inbox).

This is the load-bearing fix because Claude Code requires a user prompt to act; the launcher provides the synthetic first prompt.
close_reason: Landed adbe100 on origin/main: DEFAULT_STARTUP_PROMPT_TEMPLATE in cli.py references arm-Monitor + drain-inbox session-start sequence with {bc_name} substitution at CLI layer; explicit --startup-prompt remains total override (no concat); 71 pytest pass including 11 new unit tests covering template invariants, --help visibility, default-resolution, and total-override semantics.

title: [finding] BC agent does not autonomously arm Monitor or drain inbox at session start
description:
Observed 2026-05-28 during first live shopsystem-docs BC launch.

Setup: bc-container launch shopsystem-docs landed a healthy container with credentials, /workspace cloned, tmux 'agent' session running. User opened Claude Code inside the BC (claude in tmux). settings.json SessionStart hooks (bd prime, shop-msg prime) fired and fed orientation into Claude Code's first turn.

Expected per /workspace/CLAUDE.md (bc-primer.md canonical session-start):
1. Arm Monitor on 'shop-msg watch --bc shopsystem-docs'.
2. Drain pending inbox (shop-msg pending inbox + dispatch bc-implementer per row).
3. Then accept user direction.

Actual: agent sat at empty prompt awaiting user input. When directly asked 'have you armed the Monitor?' it answered 'No — I had not armed it yet; arming it now.' After arming it surfaced the lead-gov event already in inbox, then volunteered 'ready when you'd like me to dispatch it' — i.e., did not dispatch.

Root cause hypothesis: the bc-primer.md prose describes the session-start sequence but does not forcefully direct Claude Code to perform it as its FIRST action regardless of user prompt. SessionStart hooks deliver bd prime / shop-msg prime output as context, but Claude Code interprets the context as background, not as an imperative checklist to execute before the user speaks. The primer would need either (a) imperative MUST-DO-FIRST framing at the top, or (b) a mechanism that posts a synthetic 'first user prompt' to Claude Code instructing it to run the session-start sequence.

Three candidate fixes:
(a) Tighten bc-primer.md prose to begin with 'YOUR FIRST ACTION on every session: arm Monitor; then drain pending inbox; then await user direction. Do these before answering anything else.'
(b) bc-container launch --startup-prompt 'Run your session-start sequence per /workspace/CLAUDE.md.' (the launcher already has this flag; the docs BC was launched without it today). Adding a default startup prompt for BCs that aren't given one would force the autonomous posture.
(c) SessionStart hook posts an instruction message into the conversation directly (claude code feature?) rather than just providing context.

Live surface: any BC launched without an explicit --startup-prompt will sit idle until a human attaches and prompts. The lead-shop's reactive-router model assumes BCs autonomously process inbox; this gap breaks that assumption.

Recommendation: (a)+(b) combined. (a) makes the primer durable across launch mechanisms; (b) provides a fallback for the common case. Both small changes — (a) is in shopsystem-templates BC's bc-primer.md template; (b) is in shopsystem-bc-launcher cli.py default arg.
notes:
Pre-state verification 2026-05-29:
(b) launcher: --startup-prompt flag exists at cli.py:30 (parser) and cli.py:135 (controller wiring); default is implicit None. Repo clean on main, HEAD d02f5b9. Vehicle = request_maintenance (flat default-value change, no new capability).
(a) templates: canonical bc-primer.md at src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md has descriptive 'Session start' section at line 69 with three numbered items but lacks imperative 'YOUR FIRST ACTION' framing and lacks an explicit 'drain pending inbox' step before awaiting user direction. Existing scenarios in features/ pin substring assertions on the primer body (e.g., shop-msg watch presence) but no scenario pins 'primer leads with imperative pre-user-prompt framing.' Behavior exists but is unpinned for the imperative-framing dimension. Vehicle = request_bugfix (tightening existing prose; no new capability).
close_reason: Both child fixes landed: lead-9sq (default --startup-prompt in launcher, bc-launcher adbe100) + lead-ke1 (bc-primer.md imperative YOUR FIRST ACTION + drain step, templates 5dcc8cd). Empirical proof: docs BC now autonomously runs session-start sequence (arms Monitor + drains inbox + delegates to bc-implementer) when given the default startup-prompt. Children also bridged by lead-dl6 (symmetric lead-primer.md tightening, templates 39a64d7).

title: mechanism: shop-msg respond work_done lacks CLI-layer repeat-emit collision guard
description:
Layer-correct surface for the repeat-emit collision PO deferred from lead-cw7's template scope. The bc-reviewer / bc-implementer template precondition prose (lead-cw7, and the Implementer-template follow-up) raises the bar at the role-template level, but the CLI itself (shopsystem-messaging's shop-msg respond work_done) does NOT detect or refuse a duplicate work_done emit for the same work_id.

OBSERVED PATTERN: lead-0bw saw a second work_done emit on the same bead 2026-05-28 (per lead-cw7 description). Templates raise the bar but cannot enforce uniqueness; only the messaging CLI can see the outbox state.

CANDIDATE FIX: shop-msg respond work_done refuses (or warns + requires --force) when an outbox entry for the same (bc, work_id) already exists with status complete. Heavier hammer than template prose; lives in shopsystem-messaging.

CROSS-LINKS: lead-cw7 (Reviewer template scenarios, dispatched); lead-b6x (lead-0bw blocker, OPEN); lead-0bw (re-emit observed).

title: request_bugfix: shopsystem-bc-launcher — assert_docker_run_includes_flag step does not assert container_name against run_cmd
description: From memory note (post-lead-90z, post-lead-cyo): the assert_docker_run_includes_flag BDD step in shopsystem-bc-launcher does not assert container_name against the recorded run_cmd. This leaves a gap in launch-command pinning — a step that names a flag without binding it to the container's identity can pass even when container_name is missing or wrong. Prerequisites are now satisfied: lead-90z (isolation tightening) and lead-cyo (bc-manifest scenarios) are both CLOSED. Filed during lead-0bw reconciliation 2026-05-27. title: BC test fixtures leak synthetic 'fxhyg-' and 'test-bc-/test-lead-' rows into shop_registry without cleanup
description:
Mechanism observation lead-6nt-mech-1 (consumed 2026-05-27) flags that while lead-6nt's six scenarios pin save/restore discipline for production canonical names in repos/shopsystem-messaging/shop_registry, the meta-tests themselves seed synthetic 'fxhyg-<uuid8>-<production-name>' rows via _fxhyg_seed_production_row (tests/conftest.py:4981) and 'test-bc-<uuid12>' / 'test-lead-<uuid12>' rows via _get_or_register_bc_name / _get_or_register_lead_name (tests/conftest.py:274, 284) — none of which are cleaned up at any scope.

EMPIRICAL CONFIRMATION (architect, 2026-05-27 post-lead-6nt suite run):
  - shop-msg registry list shows 97 fxhyg-* rows and 333 total tmp_path/test-bc-/test-lead- rows accumulated from prior sessions
  - The fixture-hygiene scenarios (which only assert invariants over production canonical names) pass cleanly — narrow scope by accident

SCOPE DECISION REQUIRED (for PO):
  (a) Tighten the fixture-hygiene feature with an additional scenario: 'no test-suite-synthesized shop_registry row survives the session, period' — requires cleanup hooks for fxhyg-*, test-bc-*, test-lead-* names.
  (b) Accept the narrow scope explicitly with a comment in the feature documenting that the invariant only covers production canonical names.

The mechanism observation's proposed_action enumerates both branches. Path (a) is more honest about the broader 'hermetic test suite over shared registry' invariant; path (b) ships scope as-is and leaves the synthetic leak as a known limitation.

LOAD-BEARING for the next BC because: a future PO writing tightening scenarios about 'no test row should survive a pytest session' will need this scope answer, and right now the answer is the narrow one by accident-of-scope.

ANCHORS:
  - Source: lead-6nt-mech-1 mechanism_observation (consumed via shop-msg consume outbox)
  - Verification anchors: tests/conftest.py:4981 _fxhyg_seed_production_row; tests/conftest.py:274,284 _get_or_register_bc_name/_lead_name
  - Related: lead-6nt (production-name hygiene, closed) and lead-0bw (inbox/outbox surface, IN_PROGRESS)

title: mechanism observation: postgres-backed shop-msg state lost across LISTEN connection drop
description:
Mechanism observation logged 2026-05-27.

SYMPTOM
'shop-msg watch --lead shopsystem-product' (in-session Monitor watcher armed
at session start) died with:

  psycopg.OperationalError: server closed the connection unexpectedly
      This probably means the server terminated abnormally
      before or while processing the request.

The last event streamed before the drop was 'lead-eqx work_done'. On
operator-initiated restart of the watcher the CLI surfaced the next layer
of damage: the messaging registry was empty (7 expected entries: 2 lead
aliases — 'shopsystem-product' and 'shopsystem product' — plus 5 BCs:
shopsystem-messaging, shopsystem-templates, shopsystem-bc-launcher,
shopsystem-devcontainer, shopsystem-docs). Inbox and outbox state were
also lost: 'shop-msg pending inbox --lead "shopsystem product"' and
'shop-msg pending outbox --lead "shopsystem product"' both returned
empty, including in-flight rows that had not yet been consumed
(lead-i18 assign_scenarios and lead-bhp request_bugfix, both addressed
to shopsystem-messaging, both verified deposited earlier in the same
session via 'shop-msg read inbox').

The 7 entries were manually re-registered and the two in-flight
dispatches were re-emitted (see lead-i18 and lead-bhp notes for
wire-hash equivalence to originals).

SUSPECTED ROOT CAUSE
Postgres instance backing shop-msg lost its volume / restarted without
persistent storage. Two candidate causes:

  (a) Postgres container restart without a persistent volume mount.
      Whatever orchestration brings the messages DB up may be using
      an ephemeral / tmpfs-backed data dir, so any restart wipes
      state.

  (b) Devcontainer attached to a *different* postgres instance than
      the one it had been using. Commit 4949706 ('chore: attach lead
      devcontainer to shopsystem docker network') changed the
      devcontainer's network topology recently and may have
      inadvertently shifted DSN resolution to a different service.

To distinguish:
  - Inspect 'docker volume ls' / 'docker inspect' on the postgres
    service the lead devcontainer's SHOPMSG_DSN points to.
  - Check whether the bound volume survives container restart.
  - Compare against pre-4949706 behavior if possible.

DISTINCT FROM lead-7v1
lead-7v1 was the silent-continue watcher bug — that bug was about
the watcher NOT exiting on OperationalError and continuing to print
nothing. The fix to that bug landed and the watcher now correctly
exits on connection drop, which is what produced the observed exit
here. The new finding is about UNDERLYING DB DURABILITY, not the
watcher's exit semantics.

OBSERVATION-ONLY
Do not dispatch. File on the lead tracker so it surfaces in 'bd ready'
for follow-up triage. Likely follow-ups (not blockers):
  - Audit docker-compose / orchestration for the postgres volume mount.
  - If the volume IS persistent and was lost anyway, escalate to
    shopsystem-messaging as a request_bugfix (the BC ought to surface
    a clearer fail-fast / recovery message rather than silently
    re-bootstrapping into an empty registry).
  - If the volume is NOT persistent, file an infrastructure brief on
    the lead tracker to add one.
notes:
2026-05-27 RE-FRAMING: lead-6nt now strongly suggests the 'empty/lost registry' symptom in this bead was NOT a postgres durability problem. It was very likely the same fixture-leak corruption captured under lead-6nt — a BDD test session called `_register_shop` against production canonical names without save/restore, leaving the rows pointed at pytest tmp_paths and (depending on cleanup timing) potentially removing the lead rows entirely.

EVIDENCE FOR THE FIXTURE HYPOTHESIS (over the durability hypothesis):
  - lead-6nt directly observed active corruption today (2026-05-27) with the exact failure mode: production canonical names overwritten with tmp_path entries from pytest-200 test runs.
  - The 'manual re-registration of 7 entries' described in this bead matches exactly the 7-row repair lead-6nt completed today.
  - In-flight inbox/outbox rows for lead-i18 / lead-bhp that this bead reported as 'lost' would have been emitted into a bc=tmp_path row instead of the canonical path — so they'd still exist in the DB but would not surface in `pending` queries keyed by canonical name. Today's investigation found exactly that pattern: id=688, 690 outbox rows for lead-i18 are keyed at the dead pytest-199 tmp_path.

WHAT REMAINS UNCERTAIN:
  - The watcher exit with `psycopg.OperationalError: server closed the connection unexpectedly` was a real DB-side event. The fixture hypothesis explains the post-restart 'empty registry' but does NOT explain the connection drop itself. Possibilities: (a) genuinely independent postgres restart that coincided with a polluting test run, (b) shop-msg watch reconnect logic encountered the corrupted registry and surfaced confusing state, (c) a postgres-side maintenance event unrelated to either cause.

UPDATED PRIORITY: probably lower than P2. The 'durability' angle is now suspect; the actionable angle is the fixture hygiene captured in lead-6nt. Leaving open for now to track the watcher-exit observation separately if it recurs.

CROSS-LINK: see lead-6nt (P1 OPEN) for the active mechanism-observation and queued request_bugfix.
2026-05-27 ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE — fixture leak surfaces in inbox table too.

Monitor on the lead-shop watcher fired `lead-dual-1 work_done` from
shopsystem-messaging during a BDD test run. None of the polluting work_ids
(16 distinct rows in the lead inbox: lead-dual-1, lead-901/902/903, lead-200,
lead-301/302, lead-500/501/502, lead-col1/col2/col3, lead-001) are real beads;
all map to literal strings baked into BC feature files under
repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/.

Cleanup attempt via `shop-msg consume outbox --bc shopsystem-messaging
--work-id lead-dual-1 ...` failed because the canonical name now resolves
through shop_registry to /tmp/pytest-of-vscode/pytest-212/... — meaning the
exact registry corruption this bead originally documented was reproduced
again today, mid-session. The "empty registry after watcher restart" symptom
this bead originally captured is now firmly attributed to the BDD fixture
leak rather than to postgres durability.

NEW SIBLING BEAD: lead-0bw (P1) covers the inbox/outbox fixture surface
distinct from lead-6nt's registry surface; cross-linked.

The watcher-exit-on-OperationalError half of this bead remains an
independent open observation — no new evidence today.

title: encode ADR-010 work_done.scenario_hashes pre-emit check in BC Reviewer template (shopsystem-templates)
description:
ADR-010 (2026-05-27) makes the strict-subset rule canonical: work_done.scenario_hashes MUST be a subset of the BC's pinned @scenario_hash set in features/, AND limited to hashes carried in the resolving dispatch when on a clarify-resolution bead.

The natural place to encode the BC-side pre-emit hash-presence check is the Reviewer role template carried by shopsystem-templates. The check: before approving a work_done for emit, grep features/ for each hash in work_done.scenario_hashes; abort if any hash is absent.

This bead tracks the template edit. NOT to be dispatched to templates this turn (per parent task scope guidance — ADR clarification is the deliverable; template propagation is downstream).

Companion: the lead-architect reconciliation step (grep BC features/ for each echoed hash on work_done reception) is already implicit in the lead-architect template's @scenario_hash enumeration discipline; an explicit pass on the reconciliation side may also be warranted. Capture during template edit.
notes: dispatched assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates with 4 on-wire hashes: e0d4c445a0cd7500 (113 happy-path recompute), 50174dbb885ff5a8 (114 stale-hash block), 762e847fe873201e (115 missing-hash block), 36d22e52adcea48e (116 orphan-hash block). Feature title: 'BC reviewer template encodes ADR-010 work_done.scenario_hashes pre-emit check'. PO judgment calls addressed: scope restricted to Reviewer-emitted scenario-carrying paths (scenarios 113-115 cite assign_scenarios + non-empty request_bugfix); ADR-010 §5 origin-attribution stays architect-side (scenario 116 pins orphan-hash block as strict-subset without removed-vs-fabricated split). Pre-state: bc-reviewer.md template has zero 'scenario_hash' grep hits; BC features/ has 124 pinned @scenario_hash tags, no collisions with new on-wire hashes. Deposit verified via read inbox.
close_reason: BC dispatch landed at templates origin/main 9e7138c 'feat(lead-83l): pin bc-reviewer pre-emit scenario_hash integrity (ADR-010)'. All 4 dispatched hashes verified on-disk under features/bc_reviewer_pre_emit_scenario_hash_integrity.feature: e0d4c445a0cd7500, 50174dbb885ff5a8, 762e847fe873201e, 36d22e52adcea48e. Canonical (src/shop_templates/templates/bc-reviewer.md) and inline (.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md) byte-identical (diff exit=0). BC's adversarial probes: literal-text shortcut, step-def silent failure, adjacent partition gap — all dismissed substantively. NOTE: deferred architectural question per lead-o6tp — these scenario-prose pre-emit checks may be retired in favor of a CLI-layer 'bc-emit work-done' wrapper. Landing as authored does not block the o6tp shift; o6tp's retirement plan explicitly covers lead-83l.

title: clarify-resolution lead-1t2: dispatch tightening scenario 38 (cross-type multi-emit) to shopsystem-messaging
description:
Lead-side clarify-resolution dispatch for lead-1t2 (shopsystem-messaging).

BC's clarify (effectively a self-answer the PO ratified): the proposed tightening scenario pins success-path cross-type multi-emit (a BC emitting both work_done and mechanism_observation for the same work_id) — a distinct third category beyond single-response routing (respond_routes_to_lead_inbox) and single-type collision (respond_work_done_collision, respond_mechanism_observation_collision). Recommended placement: NEW file respond_dual_type_same_work_id.feature.

PO ratified and authored scenario at features/messaging-registry/38-bc-emits-work-done-and-mechanism-observation-for-same-work-id.gherkin. The scenario pins: given lead+BC registered and a request_maintenance with work-id 'lead-dual-1' sent, when BC runs respond work_done then respond mechanism_observation for the same work_id, then both exit zero and 'shop-msg pending inbox --lead shopsystem-product' shows both rows.

Pre-state verified empirically (2026-05-27):
- storage.py:178,186-191 already supports allow_multi_type=True for BC-to-lead writes; insert_bc_response uses it (storage.py:485,491,503). So the success path the new scenario pins is *implemented* but UNPINNED — no existing scenario asserts it.
- grep -rn '@scenario_hash' on shopsystem-messaging/features/ shows extensive coverage of single-type respond paths and single-type collision paths, but no scenario covers the cross-type-multi-emit success path. Confirmed no conflicting @scenario_hash entries to retire/supersede.

Discriminator: capability present (allow_multi_type code path), unpinned (no scenario covers cross-type success) → request_bugfix per ADR-009 vehicle (b).

The original lead-1t2 dispatch (request_bugfix carrying the collision fix) remains in flight; per its bead it is IN_PROGRESS. This new dispatch is additive — it carries scenario 38, which is the answer to the clarify the BC raised in the course of working lead-1t2. References original work_id lead-1t2 in description.

Acceptance: scenario 38 lands as a passing BDD test in shopsystem-messaging. BC emits work_done covering hash.

Vehicle on wire: shop-msg send request_bugfix --bc shopsystem-messaging --work-id <this bead ID>.
notes: Re-dispatched 2026-05-27 after postgres state-loss event (see lead-tsj). Wire payload byte-identical to original: same scenario file (no commits between original and re-dispatch), same --feature-title ('BC emits cross-type multi-response for same work_id'), same --bc-tag ('shopsystem-messaging'); --description carries a re-dispatch marker but the body's hash is computed from the wrapped scenario only, not the description.
